info.structure: Part 40: Chapter 3, Sections 4–11. Completion of the Madhu-vidyā (Honey Doctrine): the northern and upward rays, the five nectars enjoyed by the gods, and the sun that neither rises nor sets.
info.format: Devanagari, IAST, English translation, and philosophical commentaries.

sections.title: The Northern Rays — Atharva-Aṅgiras Hymns as the Bees

verses.devanagari: अथ येऽस्योदञ्चो रश्मयस्ता एवास्योदीच्यो मधुनाड्योऽथर्वाङ्गिरस एव मधुकृत इतिहासपुराणं पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha ye 'syodañco raśmayas tā evāsyodīcyo madhunāḍyo 'tharvāṅgirasa eva madhukṛta itihāsapurāṇaṃ puṣpaṃ tā amṛtā āpaḥ || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, those rays of the sun toward the north are its northern honey-cells. The Atharva-Aṅgiras hymns are the bees; the Itihāsa-Purāṇa (legend and ancient lore) is the flower; the immortal waters are the nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara continues the allegory: just as bees extract honey from flowers, the Atharva hymns extracted the luminous essence from sacred narrative. All scriptural activity culminates in the one radiant essence appearing in the sun.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Itihāsa-Purāṇa, too, is the Lord's word; its essence drawn out by meditation becomes nectar — the joy of those who serve Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva holds the Purāṇas to be a valid means of knowing Viṣṇu; their essence, like honey, is the bliss the Lord grants through right understanding.

verses.devanagari: ते वा एतेऽथर्वाङ्गिरस एतदितिहासपुराणमभ्यतपंस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्यं रसोऽजायत ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: te vā ete 'tharvāṅgirasa etad itihāsapurāṇam abhyatapaṃs tasyābhitaptasya yaśas teja indriyaṃ vīryam annādyaṃ raso 'jāyata || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: Those Atharva-Aṅgiras hymns brooded over the Itihāsa-Purāṇa; from it, thus brooded over, arose its essence — fame, splendour, vigour of the senses, virility, and food fit for eating.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The 'brooding' (tapas) is the ritual and meditative use of the texts, which releases their inner power as tangible excellence in the knower.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Through devout study the Lord causes the text's hidden glories to flow forth as blessings for the worshipper.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The fruits — fame, splendour, strength — are apportioned by Viṣṇu according to the devotion with which His word is studied.

verses.devanagari: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य परं कृष्णं रूपम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad vyakṣarat tad ādityam abhito 'śrayat tad vā etad yad etad ādityasya paraṃ kṛṣṇaṃ rūpam || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: That essence flowed forth and settled around the sun. That, verily, is what appears as the extremely dark form of the sun.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each class of texts contributes a colour to the sun's appearance; the deep dark hue is the essence of the Atharva lore. The colours are superimposed forms, not the sun's true nature — pointing to the formless reality behind all appearances.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The hues of the solar orb are glories of the Lord's body, each fed by a stream of His own revealed word.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The colours show the graded manifestation of the Lord's energies in the solar deity, who is ever distinct from and dependent on Viṣṇu.

sections.title: The Upward Rays — The Secret Teachings and Brahman as the Flower

verses.devanagari: अथ येऽस्योर्ध्वा रश्मयस्ता एवास्योर्ध्वा मधुनाड्यो गुह्या एवादेशा मधुकृतो ब्रह्मैव पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha ye 'syordhvā raśmayas tā evāsyordhvā madhunāḍyo guhyā evādeśā madhukṛto brahmaiva puṣpaṃ tā amṛtā āpaḥ || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, those rays of the sun that go upward are its upper honey-cells. The secret teachings (the Upaniṣads) are the bees; Brahman itself (the praṇava, OM) is the flower; the immortal waters are the nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Here the allegory reaches its summit: the hidden instructions — the Upaniṣads — draw their honey from Brahman itself, signified by OM. Śaṅkara notes that the highest essence comes not from ritual texts but from the knowledge portion of the Veda.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The 'secret commands' are the esoteric meditations on the Lord; their flower is the praṇava, His chief name. Their honey is the supreme bliss of union with Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The Upaniṣads, rightly interpreted, reveal Viṣṇu as the sole independent reality; their essence is the highest of all nectars because their subject is the Highest.

verses.devanagari: ते वा एते गुह्या आदेशा एतद्ब्रह्माभ्यतपंस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्यं रसोऽजायत ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: te vā ete guhyā ādeśā etad brahmābhyatapaṃs tasyābhitaptasya yaśas teja indriyaṃ vīryam annādyaṃ raso 'jāyata || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: Those secret teachings brooded over Brahman (OM); from it, thus brooded over, arose its essence — fame, splendour, vigour of the senses, virility, and food fit for eating.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Meditation on OM as taught in the secret doctrine releases the supreme essence; the listed fruits are its visible portion, while its invisible portion is liberation itself.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Contemplation of the Lord through His praṇava-name yields both worldly excellence and the supreme end — His own abode.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: OM names Viṣṇu directly; meditation on it draws forth His grace, the source of every excellence.

verses.devanagari: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य मध्ये क्षोभत इव ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: tad vyakṣarat tad ādityam abhito 'śrayat tad vā etad yad etad ādityasya madhye kṣobhata iva || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: That essence flowed forth and settled around the sun. That, verily, is what appears to tremble, as it were, in the middle of the sun.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The shimmering at the sun's heart is the subtlest essence — that of the Upaniṣadic knowledge. The word 'iva' (as it were) warns that all such appearances are provisional; the reality is the unmoving Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The trembling radiance at the centre is the most intimate glory of the Lord's solar form, perceptible to advanced meditators.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The central radiance is the special presence of Viṣṇu within the solar orb, distinct from the deity Sūrya who serves Him.

verses.devanagari: ते वा एते रसानां रसा वेदा हि रसास्तेषामेते रसास्तानि वा एतान्यमृतानाममृतानि वेदा ह्यमृतास्तेषामेतान्यमृतानि ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: te vā ete rasānāṃ rasā vedā hi rasās teṣām ete rasās tāni vā etāny amṛtānām amṛtāni vedā hy amṛtās teṣām etāny amṛtāni || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: These (essences) are the essences of essences, for the Vedas are essences and these are their essences. These are the nectars of nectars, for the Vedas are nectars and these are their nectars.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The Vedas are the essence of the world; their own essence — the luminous forms in the sun — is therefore essence doubled. Śaṅkara sees here a ladder: world → Veda → essence → the One that the essences finally indicate.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's word is the world's sweetness, and the fruit of meditating on it is sweetness redoubled — the foretaste of eternal service to Him.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The graded language ('essence of essences') confirms Madhva's hierarchy of realities, at whose summit stands Viṣṇu, the nectar of all nectars.

sections.title: The First Nectar — The Vasus with Agni

verses.devanagari: तद्यत्प्रथमममृतं तद्वसव उपजीवन्त्यग्निना मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: tad yat prathamam amṛtaṃ tad vasava upajīvanty agninā mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibanty etad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: On that first nectar the Vasus live, with Agni as their mouth (chief). Truly, the gods neither eat nor drink; they are satisfied merely by seeing that nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara explains that the gods' 'seeing' is uninterrupted contemplation; their satisfaction without eating shows that fullness comes from knowledge, not consumption — a pointer toward the self-sufficiency of the knower of Brahman.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The gods feast through vision because divine enjoyment is consciousness-borne; Agni as 'mouth' means he is their leader in enjoying the Lord's radiance.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva notes the gods' superiority to humans: their nourishment is contemplative. Yet even they depend wholly on Viṣṇu, whose essence the nectar is.

verses.devanagari: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ta etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśanty etasmād rūpād udyanti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: They enter into that very form (the red form of the sun), and from that form they rise forth.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The gods merge into and emerge from the solar colour they live upon — periodic rest and activity, mirroring the cosmic rhythm of dissolution and manifestation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Entering and rising from the form expresses the gods' intimate participation in the Lord's solar glory.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Even the gods' rest and rising are governed by the Lord within the sun; they are never independent.

verses.devanagari: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद वसूनामेवैको भूत्वाग्निनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etad evam amṛtaṃ veda vasūnām evaiko bhūtvāgninaiva mukhenaitad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśaty etasmād rūpād udeti || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: He who knows this nectar thus becomes one of the Vasus themselves; with Agni as his mouth he is satisfied by seeing that nectar; he enters that very form and rises forth from that form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Meditation conforms the meditator to its object: the knower attains the rank of the Vasus. For Śaṅkara such fruits, while glorious, remain within saṃsāra — stepping-stones toward the knowledge that liberates.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The worshipper, by the Lord's grace, is granted communion with the Vasus and shares their mode of blissful vision.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The qualified soul attains a station among the Vasus appropriate to its intrinsic grade — Madhva's doctrine of svarūpa-tāratamya (innate gradation).

verses.devanagari: स यावदादित्यः पुरस्तादुदेता पश्चादस्तमेता वसूनामेव तावदाधिपत्यं स्वाराज्यं पर्येता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa yāvad ādityaḥ purastād udetā paścād astam etā vasūnām eva tāvad ādhipatyaṃ svārājyaṃ paryetā || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: As long as the sun rises in the east and sets in the west, so long will he enjoy the sovereignty and self-rule of the Vasus.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fruit lasts a cosmic age — vast, yet bounded by time. Śaṅkara's implicit lesson: every meditational fruit short of Self-knowledge has a horizon.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: 'Svārājya' is rulership free of obstruction, conferred by the Lord for the duration of the solar order.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Sovereignty is real but derivative; the Vasus and their new companion rule only by Viṣṇu's delegation.

sections.title: The Second Nectar — The Rudras with Indra

verses.devanagari: अथ यद्द्वितीयममृतं तद्रुद्रा उपजीवन्तीन्द्रेण मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yad dvitīyam amṛtaṃ tad rudrā upajīvantīndreṇa mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibanty etad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, on that second nectar the Rudras live, with Indra as their mouth. Truly, the gods neither eat nor drink; they are satisfied merely by seeing that nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The second colour (the white form, essence of the Yajurveda) sustains the Rudras; the pattern of contemplative satisfaction repeats at each level of divine life.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Each class of gods enjoys its appointed stream of the Lord's glory, with its own leader as 'mouth'.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ordered assignments — Rudras to the second nectar, Indra as chief — reflect the immutable hierarchy established by Viṣṇu.

verses.devanagari: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ta etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśanty etasmād rūpād udyanti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: They enter into that very form, and from that form they rise forth.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: As with the Vasus, the Rudras' rest and emergence trace the rhythm of manifestation inherent in all conditioned existence.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Their entering and rising is ceaseless communion with the Lord's white radiance.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Their movements are timed and willed by the indwelling Lord.

verses.devanagari: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद रुद्राणामेवैको भूत्वेन्द्रेणैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etad evam amṛtaṃ veda rudrāṇām evaiko bhūtvendreṇaiva mukhenaitad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśaty etasmād rūpād udeti || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: He who knows this nectar thus becomes one of the Rudras themselves; with Indra as his mouth he is satisfied by seeing that nectar; he enters that very form and rises forth from that form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Knowledge assimilates the knower to the known: meditation on the second nectar wins Rudra-hood for a cosmic span.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord grants the meditator fellowship with the Rudras and participation in their vision.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Entry into the Rudra-host is according to the soul's fitness, by the Lord's appointment.

verses.devanagari: स यावदादित्यः पुरस्तादुदेता पश्चादस्तमेता द्विस्तावद्दक्षिणत उदेतोत्तरतोऽस्तमेता रुद्राणामेव तावदाधिपत्यं स्वाराज्यं पर्येता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa yāvad ādityaḥ purastād udetā paścād astam etā dvis tāvad dakṣiṇata udetottarato 'stam etā rudrāṇām eva tāvad ādhipatyaṃ svārājyaṃ paryetā || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: Twice as long as the sun rises in the east and sets in the west will it rise in the south and set in the north; and just so long will he enjoy the sovereignty and self-rule of the Rudras.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The text envisions successive cosmic epochs in which the sun's course shifts; each higher meditation yields a duration double the last — yet all remain finite.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The doubling expresses ascending grades of divine enjoyment granted to deeper meditation.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Longer sovereignty for higher hosts confirms gradation among the gods themselves.

sections.title: The Third Nectar — The Ādityas with Varuṇa

verses.devanagari: अथ यत्तृतीयममृतं तदादित्या उपजीवन्ति वरुणेन मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yat tṛtīyam amṛtaṃ tad ādityā upajīvanti varuṇena mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibanty etad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, on that third nectar the Ādityas live, with Varuṇa as their mouth. Truly, the gods neither eat nor drink; they are satisfied merely by seeing that nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The third (dark) form, essence of the Sāmaveda, sustains the Ādityas led by Varuṇa — the pattern ascends through subtler essences.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Ādityas drink the Lord's Sāma-glory through contemplative sight, Varuṇa first among them.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Each god receives according to rank; all receive from Viṣṇu.

verses.devanagari: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ta etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśanty etasmād rūpād udyanti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: They enter into that very form, and from that form they rise forth.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The recurring refrain underscores the universality of the rhythm of absorption and emergence.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Continuous communion with the Lord's dark-radiant form is their life.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Their repose and rising follow the Lord's ordinance.

verses.devanagari: स य एतदेवममृतं वेदादित्यानामेवैको भूत्वा वरुणेनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etad evam amṛtaṃ vedādityānām evaiko bhūtvā varuṇenaiva mukhenaitad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśaty etasmād rūpād udeti || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: He who knows this nectar thus becomes one of the Ādityas themselves; with Varuṇa as his mouth he is satisfied by seeing that nectar; he enters that very form and rises forth from that form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The meditator gains Āditya-hood — a higher station, a longer span, but still within the wheel of time.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Fellowship with the Ādityas is the Lord's gift to this meditation.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Attainment matches the soul's eternal grade, fixed by Viṣṇu.

verses.devanagari: स यावदादित्यो दक्षिणत उदेतोत्तरतोऽस्तमेता द्विस्तावत्पश्चादुदेता पुरस्तादस्तमेतादित्यानामेव तावदाधिपत्यं स्वाराज्यं पर्येता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa yāvad ādityo dakṣiṇata udetottarato 'stam etā dvis tāvat paścād udetā purastād astam etādityānām eva tāvad ādhipatyaṃ svārājyaṃ paryetā || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: Twice as long as the sun rises in the south and sets in the north will it rise in the west and set in the east; and just so long will he enjoy the sovereignty and self-rule of the Ādityas.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The epochs lengthen as the meditations deepen — a doubling ladder of finite glories.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Higher meditations win longer enjoyment of the Lord's delegated rule.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Duration of sovereignty is precisely meted by the Lord.

sections.title: The Fourth Nectar — The Maruts with Soma

verses.devanagari: अथ यच्चतुर्थममृतं तन्मरुत उपजीवन्ति सोमेन मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yac caturtham amṛtaṃ tan maruta upajīvanti somena mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibanty etad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, on that fourth nectar the Maruts live, with Soma as their mouth. Truly, the gods neither eat nor drink; they are satisfied merely by seeing that nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fourth (deep-dark) form, essence of the Atharva lore, sustains the storm-gods led by Soma.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Maruts taste the Lord's glory distilled from sacred narrative, with Soma at their head.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The assignment of hosts to nectars is the Lord's eternal arrangement.

verses.devanagari: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ta etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśanty etasmād rūpād udyanti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: They enter into that very form, and from that form they rise forth.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Absorption and emergence: the universal pulse of manifested life.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Their dwelling is the Lord's dark-lustrous radiance.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: All their states are Viṣṇu-directed.

verses.devanagari: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद मरुतामेवैको भूत्वा सोमेनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etad evam amṛtaṃ veda marutām evaiko bhūtvā somenaiva mukhenaitad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśaty etasmād rūpād udeti || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: He who knows this nectar thus becomes one of the Maruts themselves; with Soma as his mouth he is satisfied by seeing that nectar; he enters that very form and rises forth from that form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Marut-hood is the fruit; the ladder of finite attainments continues to lengthen.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord admits the meditator to the Maruts' company and joy.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Station among the Maruts is granted per the soul's innate worth.

verses.devanagari: स यावदादित्यः पश्चादुदेता पुरस्तादस्तमेता द्विस्तावदुत्तरत उदेता दक्षिणतोऽस्तमेता मरुतामेव तावदाधिपत्यं स्वाराज्यं पर्येता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa yāvad ādityaḥ paścād udetā purastād astam etā dvis tāvad uttarata udetā dakṣiṇato 'stam etā marutām eva tāvad ādhipatyaṃ svārājyaṃ paryetā || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: Twice as long as the sun rises in the west and sets in the east will it rise in the north and set in the south; and just so long will he enjoy the sovereignty and self-rule of the Maruts.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Each reversal of the sun's course marks a higher epoch; the doubling continues.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Longer spans of rule reward subtler contemplations.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Time itself is apportioned by the Lord to each attainment.

sections.title: The Fifth Nectar — The Sādhyas with Brahmā

verses.devanagari: अथ यत्पञ्चमममृतं तत्साध्या उपजीवन्ति ब्रह्मणा मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha yat pañcamam amṛtaṃ tat sādhyā upajīvanti brahmaṇā mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibanty etad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Now, on that fifth nectar the Sādhyas live, with Brahmā as their mouth. Truly, the gods neither eat nor drink; they are satisfied merely by seeing that nectar.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The fifth, central trembling essence — that of the Upaniṣads — nourishes the subtlest gods, the Sādhyas, with Brahmā (the praṇava or Hiraṇyagarbha) as their chief.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Sādhyas live on the innermost glory of the Lord, revealed by the secret teachings.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Even the loftiest hosts subsist on Viṣṇu's essence; 'Brahmā as mouth' marks the highest created intelligence serving the Independent One.

verses.devanagari: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: ta etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśanty etasmād rūpād udyanti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: They enter into that very form, and from that form they rise forth.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Even at the subtlest level the rhythm holds — until knowledge ends all entering and rising.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: Their life is unbroken intimacy with the Lord's central radiance.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Their states, too, depend on the Lord within the sun.

verses.devanagari: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद साध्यानामेवैको भूत्वा ब्रह्मणैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: sa ya etad evam amṛtaṃ veda sādhyānām evaiko bhūtvā brahmaṇaiva mukhenaitad evāmṛtaṃ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etad eva rūpam abhisaṃviśaty etasmād rūpād udeti || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: He who knows this nectar thus becomes one of the Sādhyas themselves; with Brahmā as his mouth he is satisfied by seeing that nectar; he enters that very form and rises forth from that form.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The highest meditational fruit in this series — Sādhya-hood — borders on the path that leads beyond all stations.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The meditator joins the innermost circle of the Lord's attendants in the cosmic order.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The soul's highest possible cosmic station is reached, yet eternal distinction from Viṣṇu remains.

verses.devanagari: स यावदादित्य उत्तरत उदेता दक्षिणतोऽस्तमेता द्विस्तावदूर्ध्वमुदेतार्वागस्तमेता साध्यानामेव तावदाधिपत्यं स्वाराज्यं पर्येता ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: sa yāvad āditya uttarata udetā dakṣiṇato 'stam etā dvis tāvad ūrdhvam udetārvāg astam etā sādhyānām eva tāvad ādhipatyaṃ svārājyaṃ paryetā || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: Twice as long as the sun rises in the north and sets in the south will it rise overhead and set below; and just so long will he enjoy the sovereignty and self-rule of the Sādhyas.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The final cosmic epoch: the sun standing overhead. Beyond this lies only the state described next — where rising and setting cease altogether.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The longest span of delegated sovereignty, prelude to the eternal state beyond all solar motion.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The last and longest finite reward before the text turns to the eternal.

sections.title: The Sun That Neither Rises Nor Sets

verses.devanagari: अथ तत ऊर्ध्व उदेत्य नैवोदेता नास्तमेतैकल एव मध्ये स्थाता तदेष श्लोकः ॥ १ ॥
verses.iast: atha tata ūrdhva udetya naivodetā nāstam etaikala eva madhye sthātā tad eṣa ślokaḥ || 1 ||
verses.english_translation: Then, having risen upward from there, it will neither rise nor set; it will stand alone in the middle. On this there is this verse:

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Śaṅkara reads this as the state of the knower: for him the sun of consciousness neither rises nor sets — the changeless Brahman, ever standing 'alone in the middle', beyond all temporal alternation.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The motionless sun symbolises the eternal, unobstructed experience of the Lord in the supreme abode, where time's alternations cease.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The ever-standing sun is the Lord's eternal splendour; for the released soul there is unbroken vision of Viṣṇu, without the rising and setting of worldly states.

verses.devanagari: न वै तत्र न निम्लोच नोदियाय कदाचन । देवास्तेनाहं सत्येन मा विराधिषि ब्रह्मणेति ॥ २ ॥
verses.iast: na vai tatra na nimloca nodiyāya kadācana | devās tenāhaṃ satyena mā virādhiṣi brahmaṇeti || 2 ||
verses.english_translation: 'There, verily, it never set, nor did it ever rise. O gods, by this truth, may I not fall from Brahman.'

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The śloka is the knower's asseveration: in the Reality there never was rising or setting at all — time itself is appearance. By this truth he prays never to fall from Brahman, i.e., never to lapse from knowledge.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The verse is the meditator's solemn oath of fidelity, invoking the changeless realm of the Lord as witness.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: An oath by the changeless world of Viṣṇu: may the devotee never be severed from Him.

verses.devanagari: न ह वा अस्मा उदेति न निम्लोचति सकृद्दिवा हैवास्मै भवति य एतामेवं ब्रह्मोपनिषदं वेद ॥ ३ ॥
verses.iast: na ha vā asmā udeti na nimlocati sakṛd divā haivāsmai bhavati ya etām evaṃ brahmopaniṣadaṃ veda || 3 ||
verses.english_translation: For him who knows this Brahma-Upaniṣad (secret doctrine of the Veda) thus, the sun neither rises nor sets; for him it is day once and for all.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: Perpetual day is the unbroken light of Self-knowledge: once ignorance is dispelled, there is no more alternation of knowledge and its loss. This is Śaṅkara's celebrated reading — liberation is not an event in time but the cessation of time's claim.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: For the liberated, the experience of the Lord is uninterrupted — a single everlasting day of communion.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Endless day means unbroken, blissful awareness of Viṣṇu in mokṣa, never again shadowed by saṃsāra.

verses.devanagari: तद्धैतद्ब्रह्मा प्रजापतय उवाच प्रजापतिर्मनवे मनुः प्रजाभ्यस्तद्धैतदुद्दालकायारुणये ज्येष्ठाय पुत्राय पिता ब्रह्म प्रोवाच ॥ ४ ॥
verses.iast: tad dhaitad brahmā prajāpataya uvāca prajāpatir manave manuḥ prajābhyas tad dhaitad uddālakāyāruṇaye jyeṣṭhāya putrāya pitā brahma provāca || 4 ||
verses.english_translation: This Brahmā told to Prajāpati, Prajāpati to Manu, Manu to his descendants. And this Brahman the father declared to his eldest son Uddālaka Āruṇi.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The lineage of transmission (sampradāya) guarantees the teaching's authority; knowledge of Brahman lives through an unbroken chain of teacher and disciple.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The sacred succession shows that this vidyā is to be received from a qualified ācārya, not invented.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Madhva prizes the guru-paramparā: truth descends from the Lord through Brahmā to the worthy.

verses.devanagari: इदं वाव तज्ज्येष्ठाय पुत्राय पिता ब्रह्म प्रब्रूयात्प्रणाय्याय वान्तेवासिने ॥ ५ ॥
verses.iast: idaṃ vāva taj jyeṣṭhāya putrāya pitā brahma prabrūyāt praṇāyyāya vāntevāsine || 5 ||
verses.english_translation: This very Brahman, then, a father may declare to his eldest son or to a worthy resident disciple —

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: The restriction protects the teaching from unfit recipients; readiness (adhikāra) is essential for knowledge to bear fruit.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The vidyā is a family treasure of the spirit, to be entrusted to the devoted and disciplined.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: Only the qualified may receive the highest doctrine — fitness is itself God-given.

verses.devanagari: नान्यस्मै कस्मैचन यद्यप्यस्मा इमामद्भिः परिगृहीतां धनस्य पूर्णां दद्यादेतदेव ततो भूय इत्येतदेव ततो भूय इति ॥ ६ ॥
verses.iast: nānyasmai kasmaicana yady apy asmā imām adbhiḥ parigṛhītāṃ dhanasya pūrṇāṃ dadyād etad eva tato bhūya ity etad eva tato bhūya iti || 6 ||
verses.english_translation: — and to no one else, even if one should offer him this whole sea-girt earth filled with wealth; for this teaching is greater than that — yes, this is greater than that.

commentaries.advaita_vedanta: No finite wealth can equal the knowledge that ends all want. The repetition seals the chapter: Brahman-knowledge is beyond price.
commentaries.vishishtadvaita: The Lord's vidyā outweighs the earth itself; it is given for love and fitness, never sold.
commentaries.dvaita_vedanta: The supreme worth of God-knowledge over all riches is affirmed twice for certainty.
